steps.title: Identify what the passage actually does
steps.content:
The passage describes Flewellen's study: she examined artifacts from a Texas site and found that Black female farmworkers used hook-and-eye closures to balance fashionable feminine silhouettes with the demands of physical labor. The final sentence states her conclusion directly. The passage is a neutral description of research findings — it does not evaluate, dispute, or celebrate the scholarship.

Now eliminate each choice:
  • Choice A — 'unexpected discovery that altered how rapidly fashions changed': the text says nothing about how quickly fashions changed, and Flewellen's finding is not framed as unexpected. ✗
  • Choice B — 'research on using fashion to resist traditional gender ideals': the text says the women balanced hegemonic ideals, not that they resisted them. ✗
  • Choice C — 'evaluate a scholarly work on urban fashion ideals': the passage describes, not evaluates, and the site is agricultural and domestic — not urban. ✗
  • Choice D — 'summarize findings of a study exploring factors influencing a fashion practice': the passage summarizes Flewellen's findings about two factors — hegemonic fashion ideals and work requirements — that shaped the women's clothing choices. ✓

steps.title: Confirm and close
steps.content:
Choice D matches what the passage does: it summarizes research into the factors that shaped a specific fashion practice among Black female farmworkers.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
